what: Vitest fails to import ESM TypeScript modules
outcome: Tests pass with native ESM resolution
learned: Vitest needs explicit ESM config: set type:module in package.json and use .js extensions in import paths even for .ts files

what: TypeScript strict mode catches null/undefined at compile time
tried: Enabled strict: true in tsconfig.json with strictNullChecks
outcome: Found 15 potential null dereference bugs before runtime
learned: Always enable strict mode in tsconfig. The upfront cost of fixing type errors is far less than debugging runtime null crashes

what: OpenClaw plugin hook throws and blocks agent message delivery
tried: Wrapped hook body in try-catch, returned void on error
outcome: Plugin errors no longer block the agent pipeline
learned: Plugin hooks must never throw. Wrap every hook body in try-catch and swallow errors — a broken plugin should degrade gracefully, not break the host

what: SQLite WAL mode improves concurrent read/write performance
tried: Set PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL on database open
outcome: Reads no longer block on writes, throughput improved significantly
learned: Always enable WAL mode for SQLite databases that serve concurrent readers and writers. Single pragma call at startup, no downside for typical use cases

what: Async error in event handler silently swallowed
tried: Added .catch() handler to every async operation in hook callbacks
outcome: Errors now logged properly, root cause visible in diagnostics
learned: Unhandled promise rejections in callbacks are silent killers. Always attach .catch() or use try-catch in async callbacks, and log to a known location

what: FTS5 query with special characters causes SQLite error
tried: Sanitized FTS5 query input by stripping operators and special chars before MATCH
outcome: All user-provided search terms work safely
learned: FTS5 MATCH queries are a mini query language — user input must be sanitized to remove AND/OR/NOT/NEAR operators, asterisks, quotes, and other special chars before use

what: Plugin accidentally logs sensitive API keys to trace output
tried: Added credential pattern redaction before any storage or logging
outcome: Keys are replaced with [REDACTED] before reaching any persistent store
learned: Sanitize at the boundary: redact credentials the moment data enters your system, not at display time. Patterns to catch: sk-*, ghp_*, AWS AKIA*, private key blocks, connection strings

what: Git rebase conflict resolution loses changes when not careful
tried: Used git rebase --interactive with fixup commits, resolved conflicts file by file
outcome: Clean linear history without lost changes
learned: During interactive rebase: resolve one commit at a time, run tests after each resolution, use git diff to verify no changes were lost. Never force-push without checking

what: SSRF vulnerability through user-controlled URL parameter
tried: Validated URL protocol must be HTTPS and hostname must not resolve to private IPs
outcome: Blocked requests to localhost, 10.x, 172.16-31.x, 192.168.x, and AWS metadata endpoint
learned: Any user-provided URL that the server will fetch must be validated: require HTTPS, reject private IP ranges (10/8, 172.16/12, 192.168/16), reject localhost, reject link-local (169.254/16), reject 0.0.0.0

what: Agent prompt injection through crafted lesson content
tried: Implemented multi-layer detection: pattern matching for injection phrases in EN/CN/JP/KR, invisible unicode detection, encoding expansion for base64/URL encoded payloads
outcome: Blocked known injection vectors across multiple languages and encoding schemes
learned: Prompt injection defense must be multi-layered: (1) pattern match common phrases in multiple languages, (2) detect invisible unicode used to hide content, (3) expand encodings (URL, base64) before scanning, (4) scan at publish boundary, redact at store boundary

what: Memory leak in long-running Node.js process from growing Map
tried: Added TTL-based eviction to in-memory caches using setTimeout cleanup
outcome: Memory usage stabilized after adding eviction policy
learned: Any in-memory cache (Map, Set, object) in a long-running process needs a size limit or TTL eviction strategy. Prefer WeakMap for object keys, or implement manual cleanup on a timer

what: ESM dynamic import fails at runtime despite TypeScript compiling
tried: Used dynamic import() with full file URL and .js extension
learned: In ESM, dynamic import paths must use .js extensions even when source is .ts. For absolute paths, convert to file:// URL using pathToFileURL(). TypeScript compile success does not guarantee runtime import success
